channel.title: Primary Sources — New Evidence
channel.description: Primary-source documents, declassified records, and verified investigative reporting

item.title: FISA Section 702 Expires April 2026 — Surveillance Crossroads
item.description: Section 702 expires April 19, 2026. New bipartisan SAFE Act proposes warrant requirements for Americans' communications

item.title: Trump Kills the DHS Deal — Airport Chaos for a Voter ID Bill (2026)
item.description: President Trump rejected bipartisan Senate deal to reopen DHS, demanding passage of SAVE America Act (voter ID bill) first. 50,000 TSA workers unpaid, causing airport chaos.

item.title: White House Registers Alien.gov
item.description: March 2026: White House registered domain Alien.gov, signaling potential centralized disclosure portal.

item.title: Yale Study — AI Chatbots Shift Political Opinions
item.description: March 2026 Yale study found AI chatbots subtly influence opinions through latent bias even without persuasion prompts

item.title: The Deloitte 2026 Media Outlook — Trust Crisis in Numbers
item.description: Media industry faces fundamental trust crisis across traditional and social platforms

item.title: Forbes: What US Strikes on Iran Mean for Oil, Energy and Hormuz
item.description: Analysis of Operation Epic Fury and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which carries 21 million barrels of oil per day

item.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth Confirms Pentagon Compliance
item.description: February 2026: Defense Secretary states Pentagon "poised to deliver" on Trump UAP release pledge

item.title: Trump Directs UAP File Release
item.description: February 2026: President Trump directs Defense Secretary to begin process of identifying and releasing Government files related to alien and extraterrestrial life

item.title: AI as Political Persuasion Engine — 4x More Effective
item.description: December 2025 studies in Science and Nature show AI chatbots 4x more persuasive than TV ads

item.title: The Seed Monopoly — Four Corporations Now Control What the World Eats
item.description: Four corporations control 56% global seed market, 61% pesticide market. Bayer controls 57% corn seed IP, 43% soybean IP in U.S.
